zotero.abstractNote: We study the problem of learning a predictor that complements a human decision-maker. In this setting, a model can either predict the label itself or defer to a human. We provide a theoretical framework for sample-efficient learning in this setting. We show that the sample complexity of learning a deferral model is related to the complexity of learning the predictor and the human's accuracy. We also provide an algorithm for learning a deferral model that is sample-efficient and achieves a low regret. We validate our theoretical findings with experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets.
